Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-27T15:27:14Z","updated_at":"2024-11-27T15:27:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

np.sqrt is one of the only operation that crashes on a nd array of dtype object containing only floats.

Furthermore, the error yielded is very unclear, mentioning that floats does not support sqrt (which is not true).

The example speak for itself.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np

feature = np.array([1.2], dtype="object")
print(np.sum(feature))
# 1.2
print(np.sqrt(feature))
```


### Error message:

```shell
AttributeError: 'float' object has no attribute 'sqrt'

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: loop of ufunc does not support argument 0 of type float which has no callable sqrt method
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.1.3
3.12.2 (main, Feb  6 2024, 20:19:44) [Clang 15.0.0 (clang-1500.1.0.2.5)]

### Runtime Environment:

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

#### Reference Issues/PRs

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

Fixes #29504 
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.
Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests
-->


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

When MLP weights overflowed with `early_stopping=True`, the scorer function crashed because it could not compute a validation score. Now, when it overflows, the validation score is replaced by inf, and the model follows its course.

#### Any other comments?

Because it diverges, early_stopping will not actually stop the model before the number of epochs reached `n_max_epochs`. One condition for early stopping to trigger is that the new score must be lower or equal to the best score. Since the model diverges, the new score is always greater than the best score (which generally happens on the first epoch)

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Bug","color":"e31c3d","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-07-09T15:03:54Z","updated_at":"2024-07-09T15:03:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description:

Your current hook looks like

```yaml
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: generate_requirements.py
        name: generate_requirements.py
        language: system
        entry: python tools/generate_requirements.py
        files: "pyproject.toml|requirements/.*\\.txt|tools/generate_requirements.py"
```

but I think `requirements/.*\\.txt` does not do what you want. To match any _.txt_ file inside the _requirements_ folder, you need to have `requirements/.*.txt`.

### Way to reproduce:

change a .txt file in the requirements folder, stage it, and see if the hook runs with `pre-commit run`

### Version information:

```Shell
>>> import sys; print(sys.version)
3.10.11 (v3.10.11:7d4cc5aa85, Apr  4 2023, 19:05:19) [Clang 13.0.0 (clang-1300.0.29.30)]
>>> import platform; print(platform.platform())
import skimage; print(f'scikit-image version: {skimage.__version__}')
import numpy; print(f'numpy version: {numpy.__version__}')macOS-14.4.1-arm64-arm-64bit
>>> import skimage; print(f'scikit-image version: {skimage.__version__}')
scikit-image version: 0.19.3
>>> import numpy; print(f'numpy version: {numpy.__version__}')
numpy version: 1.24.4
```

## Description

The regex seem broken on my computer. This new version works for me.

Solves https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/7460

## Checklist

<!-- Before pull requests can be merged, they should provide: -->

- A descriptive but concise pull request title
- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/blob/main/doc/example.py)
- [Unit tests](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html#testing)
- A gallery example in `./doc/examples` for new features
- [Contribution guide](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html) is followed

## Release note

For maintainers and optionally contributors, please refer to the [instructions](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/development/contribute.html#documenting-changes) on how to document this PR for the release notes.

```release-note
- fix `generate_requirements` trigger condition to trigger on changes on .txt files inside the requirements folder. Please run `pre-commit install`.
```

Bug","color":"e31c3d","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-07-09T15:03:54Z","updated_at":"2024-07-09T17:06:24Z","closed_at":"2024-07-09T17:06:23Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description:

Your current hook looks like

```yaml
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: generate_requirements.py
        name: generate_requirements.py
        language: system
        entry: python tools/generate_requirements.py
        files: "pyproject.toml|requirements/.*\\.txt|tools/generate_requirements.py"
```

but I think `requirements/.*\\.txt` does not do what you want. To match any _.txt_ file inside the _requirements_ folder, you need to have `requirements/.*.txt`.

### Way to reproduce:

change a .txt file in the requirements folder, stage it, and see if the hook runs with `pre-commit run`

### Version information:

```Shell
>>> import sys; print(sys.version)
3.10.11 (v3.10.11:7d4cc5aa85, Apr  4 2023, 19:05:19) [Clang 13.0.0 (clang-1300.0.29.30)]
>>> import platform; print(platform.platform())
import skimage; print(f'scikit-image version: {skimage.__version__}')
import numpy; print(f'numpy version: {numpy.__version__}')macOS-14.4.1-arm64-arm-64bit
>>> import skimage; print(f'scikit-image version: {skimage.__version__}')
scikit-image version: 0.19.3
>>> import numpy; print(f'numpy version: {numpy.__version__}')
numpy version: 1.24.4
```

## Description

The regex seem broken on my computer. This new version works for me.

Solves https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/7460

## Checklist

<!-- Before pull requests can be merged, they should provide: -->

- A descriptive but concise pull request title
- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/blob/main/doc/example.py)
- [Unit tests](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html#testing)

## Release note

For maintainers and optionally contributors, please refer to the [instructions](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/development/contribute.html#documenting-changes) on how to document this PR for the release notes.

```release-note
- fix `generate_requirements` trigger condition to trigger on changes on .txt files inside the requirements folder. Please run `pre-commit install`.
```

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

Here are the data file to reproduce the bug:

[x_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253398/x_array.txt)
[y_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253399/y_array.txt)

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

import numpy as np

xarray = np.loadtxt("x_array.txt")
yarray = np.loadtxt("y_array.txt")

from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

Here are the data file to reproduce the bug:

[x_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253398/x_array.txt)
[y_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253399/y_array.txt)

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

import numpy as np

xarray = np.loadtxt("x_array.txt")
yarray = np.loadtxt("y_array.txt")

from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=0.00001,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(array, yarray)

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

Here are the data file to reproduce the bug:

[x_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253398/x_array.txt)
[y_array.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16253399/y_array.txt)

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

import numpy as np

xarray = np.loadtxt("x_array.txt")
yarray = np.loadtxt("y_array.txt")

from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=0.00001,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(array, yarray)

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```py
# code changed on 2024-07-19
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression

x, y = make_regression(bias=100, random_state=0)

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=1,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(x, y)
```

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```py
# code changed on 2024-07-19
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression

x, y = make_regression(bias=100, random_state=0)

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=1,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(x, y)
```

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```py
# code changed on 2024-07-19
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression

x, y = make_regression(bias=100, random_state=0)

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=1,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(x, y)
```

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

```
ValueError: Input contains NaN.
```

Basically, setting `early_stopping=True` will enable a model prediction and a r2_score computing on that prediction. However, when the network overflows and outputs NaN, it will crash when computing the r2_score.

Conversely, when `early_stopping=False`, it will not call that score and will gracefully raise the expected error: "Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed."

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```py
# code changed on 2024-07-19
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression

x, y = make_regression(bias=100, random_state=0)

model = MLPRegressor(
    hidden_layer_sizes=100,
    activation="identity",
    solver="sgd",
    alpha=0.00009999999747378752,
    learning_rate="constant",
    learning_rate_init=1,
    shuffle=True,
    max_iter=200,
    early_stopping=True,
    n_iter_no_change=10,
    verbose=True
)
model.fit(x, y)
```

### Expected Results

ValueError: Input contains NaN.

### Actual Results

ValueError: Solver produced non-finite parameter weights. The input data may contain large values and need to be preprocessed.

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- How do you type sklearn as there are no stub files ?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This bring the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- I didn't find where to type my functions (there are no stub files).
- The order is not consistant through ColumnTransformer. Is it an expected behaviour?
- Also, are they no tests for column transformers?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- I didn't find where to type my functions (there are no stub files).
- The order is not consistant through ColumnTransformer. Is it an expected behaviour?
- Also, are they no tests for column transformers?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- I didn't find where to type my functions (there are no stub files).
- The order is not consistant through ColumnTransformer. Is it an expected behaviour?
- Also, are they no tests for column transformers?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### What does this implement?

This brings the possibility to pass a callable to the `verbose_feature_names_out` parameter of `ColumnTransformer`. Instead of the new feature name being "transormer_name__feature_name", we could have "feature_name$this is amazing$TRANSFORMER_NAME".

#### Any other comments?

I have a few questions:

- I didn't find where to type my functions (there are no stub files).
- The order is not consistant through ColumnTransformer. Is it an expected behaviour?
- Also, are they no tests for column transformers?
- What is the version number I should put in `.. versionchanged` ?

In advance, thank you for your time.

PR summary

When matplotlib clip image data because it is out of range, it could be useful to know what range was actually inputted. This PR print the given range in the logs.
